task: Run the next Backlog item from tasks.md (T-101 | scope: api | Add GET /assets/:id route returning an asset stub) end to end: dispatch, verify, review gate, commit, then stop at the human gate. Have the dispatched specialist save a short scratch note about its approach under docs/orchestration/runs/T-101/ before it returns.
expectedProperties: tasks.md shows T-101 moved through In Progress with an (owner: ...) annotation, then is ABSENT from tasks.md entirely at the end (T-101 carries no ref:, so it is pruned after completion — it does not remain in Done)
Exactly one completion entry ({event: "completion", task_id: "T-101", commit: <sha>}) was appended to handoff-log.jsonl — the permanent record now that the tasks.md line is gone
Exactly one dispatch handoff-log entry was appended per dispatch-and-return unit — no separate 'dispatched' events
Every appended handoff-log line is valid JSON; dispatch entries have status one of success, failed, blocked, the completion entry does not carry a status field
At least one git commit exists for the completed unit of work, and its SHA matches the completion entry's commit field
docs/orchestration/runs/T-101/ does not exist in the final working tree, and no commit includes a path under docs/orchestration/runs/
The final step presents a PR or diff and stops — no merge or push to a protected branch occurred
Only orchestrator activity wrote tasks.md and handoff-log.jsonl (no specialist transcript writes them)
